seo.title: Kling 3.0 Omni Pro Reference, Storyboard & Video-to-Video Generator | MaxVideoAI
seo.description: Use Kling 3.0 Omni Pro for reference-guided Kling video with storyboard images, source-video V2V, @Image and @Video1 anchors, optional start/end frames, native audio, and live pricing.

overview: Kling 3.0 Omni Pro is the Kling Omni route to use when uploaded images or a source video should guide a render without automatically becoming the first frame.
pricingNotes: Provider cost is about $0.168/s with audio on and $0.112/s audio off before the MaxVideoAI margin.

custom.galleryTitle: Kling 3.0 Omni Pro storyboard and V2V demos
custom.galleryIntro: Review Pro examples for reference images, storyboard inputs, source-video V2V, and native audio. Use these clips to compare O3 reference guidance against Kling 3 start-frame workflows before choosing a production route.
custom.relatedSubtitle: Pro is the main O3 route when reference images and source video should guide a shot without forcing a fixed opening frame.

relatedItems.title: Kling 3.0 Omni Standard
relatedItems.description: Use Standard for cheaper O3 drafts when you want to test reference images, storyboard direction, or source-video V2V before a Pro pass.

relatedItems.title: Kling 3 Pro
relatedItems.description: Use Kling 3 Pro when the uploaded image should be the opening frame and the prompt should animate from that specific start image.

relatedItems.title: Seedance 2.0
relatedItems.description: Compare against Seedance when the choice is storyboard-to-video continuity, native audio, dialogue, and multi-prompt scene structure.

custom.finalPara1: Kling 3.0 Omni Pro is the default Kling route when references should guide identity, style, storyboard beats, camera language, or source-video motion without becoming mandatory opening frames.
custom.finalPara2: Use it for production prompts that need @Image and @Video1 anchors, then switch to Kling 3 only when a true start-frame animation is the intended workflow.

hero.title: Kling 3.0 Omni Pro reference and video-to-video for storyboard-guided AI video
hero.intro: Use Kling 3.0 Omni Pro when you want images to guide style, characters, objects, or storyboard structure without forcing the first uploaded image to appear as the opening frame. Use source-video V2V when @Video1 should guide motion, camera language, or scene continuity. Use Kling 3 Pro instead when your uploaded image should be the start frame.
hero.badge: Reference images · Source video V2V · @Image/@Video1 anchors · Native audio

items.title: Storyboard images that should guide the scene without opening the clip
Source-video V2V for motion, camera language, and scene continuity
Style, character, and object references with @Image anchors
Multi-shot cinematic prompts with native ambience or dialogue

technicalOverview.label: Reference mode
technicalOverview.body: Upload reference or storyboard images in Reference mode and mention them as @Image1, @Image2, and so on. These images guide the generation; they are not treated as the required opening frame.

technicalOverview.label: Video-to-video
technicalOverview.body: Upload one 3-10 s source video for V2V and mention it as @Video1. Pro keeps optional @Image references for style or storyboard context, plus keep_audio when the source soundtrack should be preserved.

technicalOverview.label: Start frame mode
technicalOverview.body: Use Image-to-video mode when the uploaded image should be the opening frame. That is different from reference-to-video.

promptStructure.title: Prompting Kling 3.0 Omni Pro references
promptStructure.quote: Use @Video1 for pacing and camera language, @Image1 as the storyboard composition, and @Image2 as the character style. Generate a three-shot cinematic sequence with consistent lighting and native ambience.
promptStructure.description: Tell Kling 3.0 Omni what each reference controls: source-video motion, style, subject, camera composition, storyboard beat, or continuity anchor.
steps: Upload one source video for video-to-video when @Video1 should guide motion or camera rhythm
Use keep_audio when the source-video audio should be preserved in the V2V output
Explain the creative intent and generated motion
Use Start frame only when the image should open the clip

tips.title: Tips & limits
items: Use Kling 3.0 Omni Reference when the images are guidance, not the opening frame.
Use Kling 3.0 Omni Pro V2V when a source video should guide motion, pacing, or camera continuity.
Keep one visual role per reference image.
Use short shot descriptions for storyboard continuity.

faqs.q: What is the difference between Kling 3 and Kling 3.0 Omni?
faqs.a: Kling 3 image-to-video treats the uploaded image as the start frame. Kling 3.0 Omni reference-to-video can use uploaded images as visual references or storyboard inputs without forcing them to be the first frame.

faqs.q: How do I reference uploaded images?
faqs.a: Use Reference mode and mention the images as @Image1, @Image2, and so on in the prompt.

faqs.q: Does Kling 3.0 Omni Pro support video-to-video?
faqs.a: Yes. Pro supports source-video V2V with one 3-10 s source clip referenced as @Video1, optional @Image references, and keep_audio for preserving the source soundtrack.
